texto: Like characters in Greek tragedy, we seem fated to push technology towards its ultimate degree as if we were possessed by malignant gods. We call these gods "curiosity" and "creativity" and "reason" and "progress", but when these words are perverted by technocrats, they are more like the four horsemen of the apocalypse.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Curtis White: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a fe. O trecho “Like characters in Greek tragedy, we seem fated to push technology towards its ultimate degree as…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, future, progress pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de progress e technology.
